channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Ideas Fund Grant 2026: Funding for Innovative Nonprofits Driving Financial Inclusion, Fair Work, and Sustainability
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The Ideas Fund is now open to support nonprofit organizations with bold, innovative solutions that address some of today’s most pressing social and environmental challenges. This grant opportunity is designed to help organizations test new ideas, scale impactful initiatives, and drive meaningful change across communities. About the Ideas Fund The Ideas Fund focuses on supporting […]

The post Ideas Fund Grant 2026: Funding for Innovative Nonprofits Driving Financial Inclusion, Fair Work, and Sustainability appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Ideas Fund is now open to support nonprofit organizations with bold, innovative solutions that address some of today’s most pressing social and environmental challenges.

This grant opportunity is designed to help organizations test new ideas, scale impactful initiatives, and drive meaningful change across communities.

About the Ideas Fund

The Ideas Fund focuses on supporting nonprofits that are developing creative and practical approaches in three key areas:

Enhancing financial capability
Promoting access to fair and decent work
Strengthening the natural environment through sustainable solutions

The goal is to back ideas that are not only innovative but also scalable, sustainable, and impactful.

What the Fund Supports

Selected organizations will receive funding to:

Pilot new ideas and approaches
Test innovative solutions in real-world settings
Strengthen program impact and reach
Develop sustainable models for long-term change

Who Should Apply

This opportunity is ideal for:

Nonprofit organizations
Social enterprises with a strong social mission
Organizations working on economic inclusion, employment, or sustainability
Teams with innovative ideas ready to be tested or scaled

Why This Opportunity Matters

Many organizations have powerful ideas but lack the funding to test or implement them. The Ideas Fund bridges that gap by providing the resources needed to turn bold concepts into real-world impact.

If your organization is working to improve livelihoods, expand access to fair work, or protect the environment, this fund can help accelerate your impact.

How to Apply

Learn more and apply here.

For more Grant opportunities , Visit OFY here.

The post Ideas Fund Grant 2026: Funding for Innovative Nonprofits Driving Financial Inclusion, Fair Work, and Sustainability appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Aspen Religion Fellows 2026: $15,000 Fellowship for Emerging Leaders Advancing Pluralism
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The Aspen Institute has officially launched applications for its inaugural Aspen Religion Fellows Program, a prestigious opportunity designed for rising leaders working at the intersection of religion and society. This 18-month fellowship supports individuals committed to strengthening pluralism, dialogue, and collaboration across diverse communities. About the Fellowship The Aspen Religion Fellows Program brings together a […]

The post Aspen Religion Fellows 2026: $15,000 Fellowship for Emerging Leaders Advancing Pluralism appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Aspen Institute has officially launched applications for its inaugural Aspen Religion Fellows Program, a prestigious opportunity designed for rising leaders working at the intersection of religion and society.

This 18-month fellowship supports individuals committed to strengthening pluralism, dialogue, and collaboration across diverse communities.

About the Fellowship

The Aspen Religion Fellows Program brings together a cohort of 10 leaders aged 25–40 from across multiple sectors to explore how religion intersects with public life and global challenges.

Fellows will participate in a series of immersive convenings, including:

A week-long session at the Aspen Ideas Festival
A three-day gathering in Chicago, United States
A convening in the nation’s capital (Washington, D.C.)

Fellowship Focus Areas

Applicants can come from five key sectors:

Religion & Clerical Leadership
Religion & Climate
Religion & Media
Religion & Nonprofit Leadership
Religion & Social Trust

Benefits

$15,000 stipend to fund a self-designed pluralism project
Access to a powerful network of advisors, mentors, and fellows
Participation in high-level convenings and leadership development sessions
Opportunities to strengthen dialogue, collaboration, and leadership skills

Who Should Apply

This fellowship is ideal for:

Leaders aged 25–40
Individuals working across religion, media, climate, nonprofits, and social impact
Changemakers interested in promoting pluralism and social cohesion
Professionals seeking to expand their leadership and influence

Why This Fellowship Matters

In a world increasingly shaped by division, this fellowship equips leaders with the tools to engage across differences, foster understanding, and build stronger communities.

It’s not just about leadership, it’s about learning how to listen, collaborate, and lead with empathy in complex environments.

Application Details

Applications opened on April 2, 2026.

🔗 Learn more and apply

For more Fellowship Opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post Aspen Religion Fellows 2026: $15,000 Fellowship for Emerging Leaders Advancing Pluralism appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Mother Cabrini Health Foundation 2026 Grants: Funding Opportunity for Health-Focused Organizations in New York
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The Mother Cabrini Health Foundation has officially opened its 2026 grant application process, inviting organizations to submit a Letter of Inquiry (LOI) for funding support. This initiative is focused on advancing the health and well-being of vulnerable communities across New York, providing funding to organizations working on impactful, community-driven solutions. About the Grant Opportunity […]

The post Mother Cabrini Health Foundation 2026 Grants: Funding Opportunity for Health-Focused Organizations in New York appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Mother Cabrini Health Foundation has officially opened its 2026 grant application process, inviting organizations to submit a Letter of Inquiry (LOI) for funding support.

This initiative is focused on advancing the health and well-being of vulnerable communities across New York, providing funding to organizations working on impactful, community-driven solutions.

About the Grant Opportunity

Organizations can apply under five key program areas:

Access to Healthcare
Basic Needs
Healthcare Workforce
Mental and Behavioral Health
General Fund

Applicants are encouraged to choose the program that best aligns with their mission and project goals.

Who Can Apply

Eligible applicants include organizations that:

Align with the foundation’s mission to improve health outcomes
Serve vulnerable populations in New York
Have projects focused on healthcare access, workforce, or community well-being

Current grantees seeking renewal funding must also apply through this LOI process.

Application Timeline

LOI Submission Window: March 30 – April 23, 2026
Applicant Webinar: April 7, 2026
Full Proposal Invitations: Mid-June 2026
Grant Decisions: October and December 2026

Why This Opportunity Matters

This grant programme provides critical funding for organizations addressing pressing health challenges, from improving access to care to supporting mental health and workforce development.

For nonprofits and social impact organizations working in New York, this is a valuable opportunity to secure funding, scale impact, and reach more communities in need.

How to Apply

For more Grant opportunities, Visit OFY here

The post Mother Cabrini Health Foundation 2026 Grants: Funding Opportunity for Health-Focused Organizations in New York appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Immigrant Justice Corps 2026 Community Fellowship: Fully Funded 3-Year Opportunity for Aspiring Immigration Advocates
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The 2026 Community Fellowship by Immigrant Justice Corps (IJC) stands as one of the most impactful and prestigious opportunities for young professionals passionate about immigration justice, social equity, and legal advocacy in the United States. As the nation’s first and only fellowship program dedicated exclusively to immigration, IJC continues to bridge the justice gap by […]

The post Immigrant Justice Corps 2026 Community Fellowship: Fully Funded 3-Year Opportunity for Aspiring Immigration Advocates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The 2026 Community Fellowship by Immigrant Justice Corps (IJC) stands as one of the most impactful and prestigious opportunities for young professionals passionate about immigration justice, social equity, and legal advocacy in the United States. As the nation’s first and only fellowship program dedicated exclusively to immigration, IJC continues to bridge the justice gap by placing talented graduates into leading legal service organizations.

With applications now open for its twelfth cohort, this fully funded, three-year fellowship offers a transformative career pathway for individuals committed to supporting low-income immigrant communities while gaining hands-on legal experience.

What is the IJC Community Fellowship?

The IJC Community Fellowship is a structured, paid program designed to recruit recent college graduates and immerse them in frontline immigration advocacy. Fellows are placed within highly respected legal service organizations, where they directly support underserved immigrant populations.

Currently, IJC hosts over 130 fellows across more than 30 U.S. states, contributing significantly to expanding access to justice.

The 2026 cohort introduces a unique opportunity with three-year placements, allowing fellows to develop expertise, build professional networks, and achieve formal accreditation in immigration legal practice.

Fellowship Structure and Professional Development

The Community Fellowship is carefully designed to provide both practical experience and professional certification.

Accreditation Pathway

Fellows are expected to progress through the U.S. Department of Justice Recognition & Accreditation Program:

  • Partial Accreditation: Achieved within the first year (ideally within 6–12 months), enabling fellows to represent clients before U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services.
  • Full Accreditation: Targeted between 12–18 months, allowing fellows to practice before Immigration Courts.

This structured pathway ensures that fellows graduate with strong legal competencies and recognized credentials.

Key Responsibilities of Community Fellows

Participants in the program take on meaningful and impactful roles, including:

  • Managing individual caseloads under legal supervision
  • Providing direct legal services and representation to immigrants
  • Translating complex immigration policies into accessible language
  • Supporting communities through culturally competent advocacy
  • Engaging with diverse populations facing legal and systemic barriers

This immersive experience equips fellows with both technical legal skills and deep community engagement expertise.

Fellowship Benefits

The IJC Community Fellowship offers a comprehensive benefits package, making it one of the most competitive programs in the field:

  • Full-time salary
  • Employee benefits
  • Extensive training and mentorship
  • Hands-on legal experience
  • Professional accreditation pathway
  • Long-term career development in immigration law and advocacy

Placement Locations

For the 2026 cohort, placements are expected to be based in:

  • New York City
  • Long Island
  • Upstate New York

Fellows must be willing to relocate to their assigned location. While some placements may offer hybrid work arrangements, fully remote participation is not available.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be current undergraduate students graduating by Spring 2026 or recent graduates (no earlier than Spring 2024)
  • Hold an Associate or Bachelor’s degree
  • Be eligible for work authorization in the United States for the full duration of the fellowship
  • Demonstrate proficiency in English and at least one additional language
  • Spanish (including indigenous dialects) is strongly preferred

Applicants with DACA or TPS status are encouraged to apply.

Application Components

Candidates must submit a complete application package including:

  • Completed application form
  • Resume or CV (maximum two pages)
  • Unofficial academic transcripts
  • Statement of Interest (maximum 500 words)
  • Essay on immigration policy (maximum 500 words)
  • At least one letter of recommendation (two recommended)

The written components are critical and should reflect the applicant’s passion, cultural awareness, and commitment to immigration justice.

Selection Process

The selection process is competitive and takes place in multiple stages:

  1. Application Review (Early May 2026)
  2. Interviews (Mid-May 2026, virtual format)
  3. Final Matching Process (Late May 2026)
  4. Fellowship Agreements Issued (June 2026)

Successful applicants are matched with host organizations based on mutual preferences and program needs.

Application Deadline

All application materials, including recommendation letters, must be submitted by:

Sunday, May 3, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. (ET)

Applicants are strongly encouraged to begin early and maintain close communication with their referees to ensure timely submission.

Why Apply for the IJC Community Fellowship?

This fellowship is more than a professional opportunity—it is a platform to create meaningful social impact. Fellows become part of a national movement working to ensure equitable access to justice for immigrant communities.

By combining legal training, community engagement, and professional mentorship, the IJC Community Fellowship prepares participants for long-term careers in law, policy, and advocacy.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Immigrant Justice Corps 2026 Community Fellowship: Fully Funded 3-Year Opportunity for Aspiring Immigration Advocates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Paid Remote Climate & Conservation Finance Internship 2026 with The Nature Conservancy
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Are you looking to gain hands-on experience in climate finance, policy, and global sustainability? The Climate & Conservation Finance Policy Internship 2026 with The Nature Conservancy (TNC) is a paid, fully remote opportunity designed for students and recent graduates eager to work at the intersection of finance, policy, and environmental impact. Over eight weeks, interns […]

The post Apply Now: Paid Remote Climate & Conservation Finance Internship 2026 with The Nature Conservancy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you looking to gain hands-on experience in climate finance, policy, and global sustainability? The Climate & Conservation Finance Policy Internship 2026 with The Nature Conservancy (TNC) is a paid, fully remote opportunity designed for students and recent graduates eager to work at the intersection of finance, policy, and environmental impact. Over eight weeks, interns will contribute to real-world projects, support global initiatives, and build valuable professional skills. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submission is strongly encouraged.

About the Opportunity

The Nature Conservancy (TNC) is one of the world’s leading environmental organizations, working to protect lands and waters globally. Through its Internships @TNC program, the organization offers structured, paid opportunities for emerging professionals to apply their academic knowledge to real-world conservation challenges.

This specific internship is part of the Climate and Conservation Finance Policy (CCFP) team, which focuses on financing climate and nature solutions in emerging markets and developing economies. The team works with governments, financial institutions, and global stakeholders to design and implement innovative financial mechanisms that support sustainability and economic resilience.

Interns in this role will contribute to projects that explore tools such as debt-for-nature swaps, green finance frameworks, ecosystem service payments, and climate-related financial strategies. They will also support research and policy development aimed at integrating nature and biodiversity into economic systems and financial decision-making.

The internship runs for 8 weeks, from 15 June to 7 August 2026, and is conducted remotely. Interns will participate in onboarding, training, mentorship, and networking activities, while also completing a capstone project that showcases their learning and contributions.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Climate change and environmental degradation are among the most pressing global challenges today. Addressing these issues requires innovative financial solutions that can support sustainable development while protecting natural ecosystems.

The work of the CCFP team at The Nature Conservancy is critical in advancing these solutions by helping governments and institutions design financial systems that integrate environmental priorities. This internship allows participants to contribute to efforts that have real-world policy and economic implications, particularly in developing regions where the need for sustainable solutions is urgent.

By joining this program, interns gain insight into how global environmental strategies are developed and implemented, while also building the skills needed to contribute to long-term sustainability goals.

What You’ll Gain

This internship offers a strong foundation for careers in sustainability, policy, and international development:

  • Paid Experience: Earn $22 per hour while gaining professional experience in a global organization.
  • Real-World Impact: Work on projects that influence climate and conservation finance strategies worldwide.
  • Skill Development: Build expertise in research, policy writing, data analysis, and stakeholder engagement.
  • Mentorship and Training: Receive guidance from experienced professionals and participate in structured learning sessions.
  • Global Exposure: Contribute to international initiatives and support major events such as climate and biodiversity forums.

This opportunity is particularly valuable for individuals aiming to enter careers in climate policy, development finance, or environmental economics.

Eligibility

To be eligible for this internship, applicants must:

  • Be currently enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate program, or be a recent graduate (within six months)
  • Have a background in policy, finance, economics, environmental studies, or related fields
  • Demonstrate experience in research, writing, and communication
  • Be fluent in English

Preferred qualifications include:

  • Experience in climate finance, international development, or public policy
  • Familiarity with data analysis or visualization tools
  • Ability to work independently in a remote and collaborative environment
  • Knowledge of additional languages such as Spanish, Portuguese, or French

Application Process and Deadline

To apply for the Climate & Conservation Finance Policy Internship:

  1. Prepare Your Application: Update your CV and ensure your experience aligns with the role requirements.
  2. Apply Online: Submit your application through The Nature Conservancy’s official careers portal.
  3. Rolling Review: Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and the position may close once a strong candidate pool is identified.

Internship Duration: 15 June – 7 August 2026
Location: Remote
Compensation: $22/hour

To learn more and apply, visit the official Nature Conservancy internship job posting.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply Now: Paid Remote Climate & Conservation Finance Internship 2026 with The Nature Conservancy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Susan Carroll Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Investigative Journalism Opportunity for Emerging Reporters
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The Susan Carroll Fellowship, offered by the Investigative Reporters and Editors (IRE), is a prestigious and highly competitive opportunity designed to empower emerging journalists with the skills, mentorship, and financial support needed to excel in investigative reporting. Named in honor of the late investigative journalist Susan Carroll, this fellowship aims to continue her legacy of […]

The post Susan Carroll Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Investigative Journalism Opportunity for Emerging Reporters appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Susan Carroll Fellowship, offered by the Investigative Reporters and Editors (IRE), is a prestigious and highly competitive opportunity designed to empower emerging journalists with the skills, mentorship, and financial support needed to excel in investigative reporting. Named in honor of the late investigative journalist Susan Carroll, this fellowship aims to continue her legacy of impactful, data-driven journalism that holds institutions accountable and amplifies underreported stories.

This fellowship is particularly significant in today’s media landscape, where investigative journalism plays a crucial role in uncovering truths, promoting transparency, and driving societal change. The program is structured to support early-career journalists by providing them with access to training, resources, and a network of experienced professionals in the field.

Overview of the Susan Carroll Fellowship

The Susan Carroll Fellowship is designed for journalists who are passionate about investigative reporting but may lack access to the resources and training needed to fully develop their potential. Through this fellowship, selected participants receive financial assistance to attend IRE’s renowned training programs, where they can gain hands-on experience and learn advanced investigative techniques.

The fellowship places a strong emphasis on data journalism, a field that Susan Carroll was deeply committed to. Participants are trained to analyze complex datasets, uncover hidden patterns, and transform raw information into compelling stories that inform and engage the public.

What the Fellowship Offers

One of the most attractive aspects of the Susan Carroll Fellowship is its comprehensive support package. Fellows receive funding that covers the cost of attending IRE training events, including registration fees, travel expenses, and accommodation. This ensures that financial constraints do not hinder talented journalists from accessing world-class training.

In addition to financial support, fellows gain access to:

  • Intensive investigative journalism training sessions
  • Workshops on data analysis, reporting techniques, and storytelling
  • Networking opportunities with leading journalists and media professionals
  • Mentorship from experienced investigative reporters
  • Exposure to cutting-edge tools and technologies used in modern journalism

These benefits collectively create a transformative learning experience that equips fellows with the skills needed to produce impactful investigative stories.

Training and Learning Experience

The fellowship provides an immersive learning environment where participants engage in practical, hands-on training. Workshops are led by industry experts who share real-world insights and best practices. Topics typically include:

  • Data collection and cleaning
  • Statistical analysis and visualization
  • Freedom of information requests
  • Ethical considerations in investigative journalism
  • Digital security for journalists

By the end of the program, fellows are expected to have a deeper understanding of investigative reporting processes and the confidence to undertake complex journalistic projects.

Application Process and Deadline

Applicants are required to submit a detailed application that demonstrates their interest in investigative journalism, their career goals, and how the fellowship will help them achieve those goals. Supporting materials may include writing samples, a resume, and references.

Deadline: The application deadline varies annually. Applicants are strongly encouraged to check the official fellowship page and apply early to avoid missing out on this opportunity.

Why You Should Apply

The Susan Carroll Fellowship is more than just a training program; it is a career-defining opportunity for aspiring investigative journalists. It not only enhances technical skills but also provides a platform for fellows to connect with a global community of professionals who are dedicated to making a difference through journalism.

For early-career journalists, this fellowship can serve as a stepping stone to greater opportunities, including positions in leading media organizations, collaborations on high-impact investigations, and recognition within the journalism community.Moreover, the fellowship’s focus on inclusivity ensures that journalists from diverse backgrounds have the opportunity to participate and contribute their unique perspectives to the field.In an era where credible information is more important than ever, initiatives like the Susan Carroll Fellowship play a vital role in strengthening the field of investigative journalism. By investing in the next generation of reporters, the fellowship helps ensure that important stories continue to be told and that accountability remains a cornerstone of society.

If you are passionate about uncovering the truth and making a meaningful impact through journalism, the Susan Carroll Fellowship is an opportunity you should not overlook.

Deadline: Varies annually (apply early)

APPLY HERE 

For more information on this opportunity, click here. 

For more fellowship opportunities, click here .

The post Susan Carroll Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Investigative Journalism Opportunity for Emerging Reporters appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Empowering Women for Climate Action: Sustainable Livelihoods and Community Resilience.
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Across the globe, the intersection of climate change and gender inequality continues to shape the lives of millions. Addressing these challenges requires innovative, inclusive, and community-driven solutions. At the forefront of this movement is SCWEI, an organization committed to empowering women while advancing sustainable climate action. About the Initiative SCWEI is dedicated to promoting sustainable […]

The post Empowering Women for Climate Action: Sustainable Livelihoods and Community Resilience. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Across the globe, the intersection of climate change and gender inequality continues to shape the lives of millions. Addressing these challenges requires innovative, inclusive, and community-driven solutions. At the forefront of this movement is SCWEI, an organization committed to empowering women while advancing sustainable climate action.

About the Initiative

SCWEI is dedicated to promoting sustainable climate solutions while uplifting underprivileged women. By integrating environmental action with economic empowerment, the organization is creating pathways for women to lead, thrive, and contribute meaningfully to their communities.

Mission

“To empower underprivileged women as leaders in climate resilience and sustainability, equipping them with essential skills and resources for dignified livelihoods and economic security. Through our initiatives, we bridge environmental action with economic opportunity, creating communities that thrive in harmony with nature and equality.”

Vision

“A sustainable world led by empowered women, where communities protect the environment and achieve lasting economic security, creating a future of equal opportunity, ecological balance, and shared prosperity.”

Launch of the Community-Based Female Tailoring Training Center

As part of its commitment to women’s empowerment, SCWEI has launched a Community-Based Female Tailoring Training Center. This initiative is designed to equip women with practical, income-generating skills that foster independence and long-term economic stability.

The center recently hosted its first training session, attracting a strong and enthusiastic group of participants. The session was marked by a spirit of collaboration, determination, and hope, as women began their journey toward self-reliance and financial empowerment.

This milestone reflects SCWEI’s broader goal of creating inclusive opportunities where women can transform their skills into sustainable livelihoods. By supporting women at the grassroots level, the organization is strengthening community resilience and promoting economic inclusion.

Driving Sustainable Climate and Women Empowerment

SCWEI’s work goes beyond skills training. The organization is deeply committed to advancing sustainable climate practices while empowering women through:

  • Education and Capacity Building: Providing women with knowledge and tools to adapt to climate challenges.
  • Advocacy: Promoting gender equality and environmental sustainability at local and global levels.
  • Community Initiatives: Implementing projects that enhance climate resilience and improve livelihoods.

These efforts contribute to building communities that are both environmentally sustainable and socially equitable.

Join the Movement

Climate change and gender inequality are interconnected challenges that require collective action. SCWEI invites individuals, partners, and communities to join hands in addressing these pressing issues.

  • Support initiatives that empower women through skills and education
  • Advocate for climate resilience and sustainable practices
  • Participate in programs that drive community transformation

Together, we can create a future where women lead climate action, communities thrive sustainably, and opportunities are accessible to all.

Click Here to get involved.

Empowering women is not just a social priority—it is a critical strategy for achieving sustainable development and climate resilience. Through initiatives like the Community-Based Female Tailoring Training Center, SCWEI is demonstrating how targeted interventions can transform lives and strengthen communities.

By bridging the gap between environmental sustainability and economic opportunity, the organization is paving the way for a more inclusive, balanced, and prosperous world.

Discover more similar opportunities Here.

The post Empowering Women for Climate Action: Sustainable Livelihoods and Community Resilience. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Win $1 Million: Apply for the Yass Prize 2026 for Education Innovators in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Are you building a solution that is transforming education in the United States? The Yass Prize 2026 is now open, offering one of the most prestigious and competitive funding opportunities for education innovators. With a $1 million grand prize and access to a high-impact accelerator, this program is designed to support organizations that are reshaping […]

The post Win $1 Million: Apply for the Yass Prize 2026 for Education Innovators in the U.S. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you building a solution that is transforming education in the United States? The Yass Prize 2026 is now open, offering one of the most prestigious and competitive funding opportunities for education innovators. With a $1 million grand prize and access to a high-impact accelerator, this program is designed to support organizations that are reshaping how education is delivered and experienced. If you are an edtech founder, school leader, or nonprofit innovator, this is your chance to scale your impact and gain national recognition. Applications must be submitted by 1 June 2026.

About the Opportunity

The Yass Prize is widely recognized as one of the leading awards for education innovation in the United States. Often referred to as the “Pulitzer for Education Innovation,” the program aims to identify and support organizations that are creating meaningful change in education systems.

The initiative is built around the concept of STOP education—solutions that are Sustainable, Transformational, Outstanding, and Permissionless. These principles guide the selection of organizations that demonstrate strong potential to improve education outcomes while expanding access and opportunity.

At the heart of the program is the Yass Prize Accelerator, a structured and immersive experience designed to help selected organizations refine their ideas, strengthen their strategies, and prepare for growth. Participants in the accelerator engage in a bootcamp that includes mentorship, training sessions, and exposure to leaders across education, policy, and business sectors.

Throughout the program, participants will work on improving their business models, enhancing their storytelling, and preparing to pitch their solutions to a wider audience. The accelerator culminates in a competitive process where participants present their work and compete for the grand prize funding.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Education systems worldwide are undergoing rapid change, and innovation is essential to meet the needs of diverse learners. The Yass Prize plays a critical role in supporting organizations that are pushing boundaries and rethinking traditional approaches to education.

By providing both funding and strategic support, the program enables innovators to test, refine, and scale their ideas, ultimately reaching more students and communities. It also fosters collaboration among leaders across sectors, creating a shared ecosystem of innovation and impact.

This opportunity is especially important because it recognizes and supports solutions that not only improve academic outcomes but also promote dignity, inclusion, and long-term success for learners.

What You’ll Gain

Participating in the Yass Prize program offers significant benefits:

  • Major Funding Opportunity: Compete for the $1 million grand prize, with additional funding opportunities available.
  • Accelerator Experience: Join an intensive bootcamp focused on strategy, growth, and impact.
  • Expert Guidance: Receive personalized feedback from industry experts and communication specialists.
  • High-Level Exposure: Present your work to leaders across education, policy, and business sectors.
  • Long-Term Network: Become part of a lifelong community of education innovators and changemakers.

This opportunity is particularly valuable for organizations seeking to scale their solutions and attract investment or partnerships.

Eligibility

The Yass Prize is open to organizations and founders working in education within the United States. Eligible applicants include:

  • EdTech companies
  • Schools and microschools
  • Nonprofit organizations
  • Education-focused startups and initiatives

Applicants should be developing innovative, scalable solutions that improve education outcomes and expand access to quality learning.

The program is specifically focused on organizations operating within the United States, and applicants should demonstrate a strong commitment to transforming education systems.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply for the Yass Prize 2026:

  1. Review Eligibility: Ensure your organization meets the program criteria.
  2. Complete the Application: Submit your application through the official platform.
  3. Prepare for the Accelerator: Shortlisted applicants will be invited to participate in the Yass Prize Accelerator.

Application Deadline: 1 June 2026 (12:00 PM EST)

To learn more and apply, visit the official Yass Prize website. 

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Win $1 Million: Apply for the Yass Prize 2026 for Education Innovators in the U.S. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: CBAI Summer AI Safety Fellowship 2026 in the USA (Fully Funded)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Cambridge Boston Alignment Initiative (CBAI) Summer Research Fellowship in AI Safety 2026 is a prestigious, fully funded program designed for aspiring researchers who want to build impactful careers in artificial intelligence safety. Taking place in Cambridge, Massachusetts, this intensive nine-week fellowship runs from June 8 to August 10, 2026, offering participants the opportunity to […]

The post Apply Now: CBAI Summer AI Safety Fellowship 2026 in the USA (Fully Funded) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Cambridge Boston Alignment Initiative (CBAI) Summer Research Fellowship in AI Safety 2026 is a prestigious, fully funded program designed for aspiring researchers who want to build impactful careers in artificial intelligence safety. Taking place in Cambridge, Massachusetts, this intensive nine-week fellowship runs from June 8 to August 10, 2026, offering participants the opportunity to work at the forefront of AI safety research.

About the Fellowship

The program brings together talented individuals to explore critical areas in AI safety, including:

  • Interpretability of AI systems
  • Multi-agent safety
  • Formal verification
  • Risk management frameworks
  • AI governance and policy

Fellows collaborate closely with experienced mentors and dedicated in-house research managers, gaining hands-on experience in tackling real-world AI safety challenges.

In addition to research work, participants engage in weekly workshops, speaker sessions, and community events, connecting with experts and peers across top institutions such as Harvard, MIT, Northeastern University, and leading AI safety organizations.

Fellowship Impact

The inaugural cohort has already demonstrated impressive outcomes, including:

  • Securing roles at leading AI safety organizations like Goodfire and Redwood
  • Publishing research accepted at top conferences such as NeurIPS and ICLR
  • Establishing independent research groups
  • Sharing policy-relevant insights with decision-makers in Washington, D.C.

This highlights the fellowship’s strong track record in launching successful AI safety careers.

What the Fellowship Offers

Participants will receive a comprehensive support package, including:

1. Generous Stipend

  • $10,000 provided over the nine-week program

2. Accommodation Support

  • Housing arranged for fellows coming from outside the Boston Metropolitan Area (via Harvard dorms or Airbnb)

3. Dedicated Workspace

  • 24/7 access to office space in Harvard Square, just minutes from Harvard Yard

4. Mentorship & Research Guidance

  • High-touch support from in-house research managers
  • Guidance to refine research direction and strengthen technical skills

5. Professional Development

  • Structured opportunities to enhance research capabilities and career growth

6. Networking Opportunities

  • Access to a vibrant AI safety community across top universities and organizations

7. Research Support

  • Resources and collaboration opportunities to produce impactful work

8. Extension Fellowship Opportunities

  • Potential pathways to continue research beyond the program

Why You Should Apply

If you’re passionate about shaping the future of artificial intelligence and ensuring its safe development, this fellowship offers a rare chance to:

  • Gain hands-on research experience in AI safety
  • Work alongside leading experts in the field
  • Build a strong professional network
  • Accelerate your career in a rapidly growing and impactful domain

Application Deadline

Deadline: April 12, 2026

APPLY HERE

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early to secure a spot in this highly competitive program.

This fellowship is ideal for students, recent graduates, and early-career researchers who are committed to addressing some of the most pressing challenges in AI development and governance.

Discover more similar opportunities here. 

The post Apply Now: CBAI Summer AI Safety Fellowship 2026 in the USA (Fully Funded) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon 2026: Build, Innovate, and Compete for $30,000+ in Prizes
item.dc:creator: Akua Ntim
item.description:
The rapid evolution of artificial intelligence has created unprecedented opportunities for developers, innovators, and creators worldwide. One of the most exciting platforms driving this innovation is Hack-Nation’s Global AI Hackathon 2026, a high-impact international event designed to bring together some of the brightest minds in technology to build real-world AI solutions in just 24 hours. […]

The post Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon 2026: Build, Innovate, and Compete for $30,000+ in Prizes appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The rapid evolution of artificial intelligence has created unprecedented opportunities for developers, innovators, and creators worldwide. One of the most exciting platforms driving this innovation is Hack-Nation’s Global AI Hackathon 2026, a high-impact international event designed to bring together some of the brightest minds in technology to build real-world AI solutions in just 24 hours.

Hack-Nation is an initiative rooted in the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) ecosystem, with a mission centered on fostering innovation, collaboration, and rapid product development. As a nonprofit organization, it connects global talent from students to experienced developers and empowers them to turn ideas into functional prototypes under real startup-like pressure.

What is the Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon?

The Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon is an intensive, fast-paced innovation competition where participants collaborate to design, develop, and launch AI-powered solutions within a 24-hour sprint. Unlike traditional competitions, this hackathon emphasizes execution, creativity, and real-world application over theoretical concepts.Participants will work individually or in teams to solve open-ended challenges using artificial intelligence, machine learning, automation tools, and modern web technologies. The event is structured to simulate real startup environments, where speed, innovation, and adaptability are critical.The 2026 edition continues to expand its global footprint, attracting participants from over 60 countries and connecting them through both physical hubs and virtual platforms.

Why You Should Participate

Participating in the Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon offers far more than just competition. It is a gateway to global exposure, skill development, and career advancement.

Access to Significant Prizes

Participants compete for over $30,000 in prizes, including cash rewards and OpenAI API credits. Additionally, more than $150,000 worth of API credits will be available during the hackathon, enabling participants to build powerful and scalable AI solutions.

Real-World Experience

This hackathon is designed to mimic startup conditions. Participants experience the pressure of building and shipping products within tight deadlines, preparing them for real-world tech and entrepreneurial environments.

Mentorship and Guidance

Throughout the event, participants receive guidance from experienced mentors, including professionals from leading organizations such as Google and OpenAI. This mentorship enhances both technical and strategic thinking.

Global Networking Opportunities

Hack-Nation connects a diverse community of developers, designers, and innovators. Participants get the opportunity to collaborate with talented individuals from around the world, forming meaningful professional relationships.

Career and Startup Opportunities

Top-performing teams may gain access to further opportunities such as venture incubation programs, investor exposure, and startup support initiatives. Previous participants have gone on to join accelerator programs and build successful AI ventures.

Event Timeline

Hackathon Dates: April 25 – April 26, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early, as selection is competitive and spots are limited.

Who Can Participate?

The Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon is open to individuals who meet the legal age requirement in their country of residence. It welcomes participants from nearly all countries and territories, making it a truly global event.Whether you are a beginner exploring AI or an experienced developer building advanced systems, the hackathon provides an inclusive platform for all skill levels.

What You Will Build

Participants will focus on creating innovative AI-driven solutions that address real-world challenges. These may include AI-powered automation tools, healthcare and data-driven solutions, intelligent agents and assistants, business and productivity applications, and creative AI platforms.The hackathon encourages open-ended problem-solving, giving participants the freedom to explore ideas that have real impact.

How to Apply

To participate, applicants must complete the online registration form through the official platform.

Due to the competitive nature of the hackathon, applicants are advised to present strong technical skills, creativity, and a clear interest in AI-driven innovation.

The Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon 2026 is more than just a competition;it is an opportunity to challenge yourself, collaborate with global talent, and build solutions that could shape the future.With substantial prizes, world-class mentorship, and access to a global innovation network, this hackathon stands out as one of the most impactful AI events of the year.

Application Deadline: April 11, 2026

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, click here .

For more opportunities on competitions , click here .

The post Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon 2026: Build, Innovate, and Compete for $30,000+ in Prizes appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
